text:
User: I also need a tenex-edge mgmt session list that will show me all sessions, allow me to toggle on/off some of them, navigate up/down, fuzzy search, and kill sessions [...] - Delete `agents` and top-level `tui` completely—no aliases. [...] - One canonical daemon query joining session records, memberships, lifecycle, and endpoints. The current TUI’s `agents_list_sessions` + `pty_status` client merge cannot reliably represent all sessions. [...] Assistant: The agent has confirmed the same root cause and has chosen the right boundary: a daemon-owned `operator_sessions` projection, then `mgmt session list` on top of it. That avoids papering over the current client-side merge bug. [...] - Old top-level `tenex-edge tui` completely removed [...] - Top-level `agents` is deleted completely, with no aliases. [...] User: I expected the mgmt session list to be like a inquirer type thing -- not a full tui [...] Assistant: You’re right—the current implementation is an alternate-screen application, while your request was an inline Inquirer-style picker. I’m pivoting to correct that first: the command should stay in the normal terminal flow, show a compact filterable checklist, let you toggle sessions and kill the selection, then exit cleanly. After that lands, I’ll resume the `my session` command work.

Assistant: I found the mismatch in code: it explicitly enters the alternate screen and carries pane attachment/input forwarding—far beyond the checklist interaction you described. The correction will delete that whole session-TUI layer and use the repository’s existing `inquire` prompt stack: type-to-filter, arrows to move, Space to toggle, Enter to submit, then an explicit kill confirmation. [...] Assistant: The live smoke now matches the intended interaction: compact one-line rows, inline fuzzy filtering, Space toggles checkboxes, Enter summarizes the selection, and the kill confirmation defaults to No. I also verified cancellation leaves the terminal in normal mode and performed no kill. I’m at the full repository gates now. [...] Assistant: The corrective change is complete and all local gates pass: 1,103 unit tests, clippy with warnings denied, formatting, LOC, and helper-import checks. The diff removes roughly 1,500 lines of alternate-screen/pane machinery and replaces it with the compact inline picker. I’m publishing the PR now and will verify its exact head/file scope before merging.
